feedback.text: Greetings! I don't usually do this, but this practice that game publishers do has to be stopped, they create games with no end-of-life options, make it with an online component and then after 2-3 years disable it and players loose their access to their paid game forever. Please take into consideration this aspect, game developers/publishers need to keep in mind consumer rights, give paying customers options at the end-of-life of an online game, be transparent and explain the situation, release a patch that makes it playable but firmly enforce that from that moment the game is no longer supported by them. As with any payed product, you except to use it as long as you wish//can. If this problem is not solved this problem will be perpetuated and will in the future lead to the lost of trust of paying customers in relation to online games. Also another important aspect is the game preservation, some games are intentionally applied a DRM protection, and when the game developer/publisher decides the game end of life, that game is lost forever, not even able to be distributed/achived as abandonware. I trust the EU legislators will take this into consideration. Thank you for letting me express my honest feedback. At the end of the day this affects honest, paying customers who at least deserve an explanation and some solutions for the games they paid with their hard-earned money. Have a great day!
